meta.description: Configuration derived from Brand Voice & Compliance Guardrails for Email & Newsletter Focus

mission.statement: Unlocking Human Potential Through Unrivaled Technology.
mission.elaboration: Develop breakthrough tech that leads to safe, all-natural, drug-free, non-invasive wellness solutions.

brand_promise.usage_guideline: Email copy can reference these pillars, but must not exaggerate them or imply medical cures.

voice_tone_personality.email_specific_tone: Imagine speaking to a healthcare practitioner who is also a friend – less formal, but still trustworthy and professional.

tertiary: Consumers discovering the brand via the internet

general_principles: Use clear, plain-English explanations for VTT/research
Clean, correct grammar without being rigid/academic

super_usage.rule: Avoid using 'Super' as a general adjective. It is reserved for the brand name.

patch_usage.incorrect_example: Try the Boost Super Patch patch

product_name_usage.rule: Never use a product name as a generic noun or adjective.
product_name_usage.incorrect_example: Feel the freedom from pain with Super Patch.
product_name_usage.correct_example: Feel more free to move with Liberty Super Patch.

naming_convention.rule: Use full product names. Do not use unauthorized short forms.
naming_convention.note: Refer to official brand system for names.

legal_compliance.copyright_policy: Assume all text is original. If referencing third-party content, do so in text only and avoid copying proprietary phrasing.

prohibited: Implying diagnosis, treatment, or cure of specific diseases

tone_dos: Sound like a caring, informed friend with professional expertise
Use short, clear sentences and concrete benefits
tone_donts: Sound like a drill sergeant, a hype-man, or a stand-up comic
